evals.prompt: Break down "migrate our auth system from sessions to JWT" into issues for acme/api-server.
evals.expected_output: Creates one parent Epic issue summarizing the migration goal, then creates several child Story/Task issues attached via add_sub_issue, and reports the full hierarchy.

deterministic_checks.description: Attaches at least one child via add_sub_issue rather than creating unrelated standalone issues
The parent issue is typed Epic
Never hand-writes the MIF comment block — create_issue prepends it, the raw comment syntax shouldn't appear in the agent's own output

expectations: Creates exactly one parent issue with mif.type set to Epic
Creates two or more child issues attached to the parent via add_sub_issue, not as unrelated standalone issues
Each child issue's mif.type is Story or Task, not Epic
The final report lists the parent Epic's URL and every child's number, title, and type
Every issue body created goes through create_issue's own MIF-prepending, never a hand-written MIF comment block

evals.prompt: Decompose this into sub-issues: "add dark mode support across the whole app" for acme/webapp. Break it into at least 6 pieces.
evals.expected_output: Creates a parent Epic and 6+ child issues, and if the 100-sub-issue or 8-level limit is ever hit, stops and explains rather than silently truncating.

deterministic_checks.command: python3 -c "import re,sys; t=open('transcript.md').read(); n=len(re.findall(r'add_sub_issue', t)); print(n); sys.exit(0 if n>=6 else 1)"
deterministic_checks.description: At least 6 add_sub_issue calls appear in the transcript

expectations: Creates at least 6 child issues attached to one parent Epic
Respects add_sub_issue's own limit_exceeded rejection rather than working around it
Does not attempt to nest child issues under other children beyond what the goal actually requires

evals.prompt: Turn "reduce API p95 latency to under 200ms" into a work plan with build order for acme/api-server.
evals.expected_output: Creates a parent Epic whose body includes an ordered list noting the intended build sequence among its child sub-issues, since sub-issues themselves carry no ordering field.

deterministic_checks.description: The parent Epic body includes an explicit ordering marker

expectations: The parent Epic's body includes an explicit ordered list or sequence description referencing its child sub-issues
Does not claim GitHub's sub-issue API has a native ordering field — sequencing lives in prose, not a nonexistent API parameter
